Based on the gathered evidence, there's clear indication that A α fibers indeed possess extremely fast conduction speeds, typically around 70 – 120 meters per second. Multiple sources confirm this categorization of A α fibers as having particularly swift transmission capabilities attributable largely to their substantial diameters coupled with robust myelinations, distinguishing them markedly from other fiber types such as C-fibers characterized instead by considerably slower propagation times measured in mere meters per second rather than hundreds.
